detail: Issue #394 claimed: status:available -> status:in-progress
preflight passed against /home/dima/dev/vexa-pack-pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd after stashing untracked evidence
branch codex/pack-pack-msteams-diarization-cutover at /home/dima/dev/vexa-pack-pack-msteams-diarization-cutover off main@0360466
npm install --ignore-scripts --workspace=core; added @huggingface/transformers ^4.2.0; pre-existing node-gyp Zoom-native build failure side-stepped
copied 5 files from predecessor rnd/diarization/src/ to core/src/services/diarization/; CJS adaptation (dropped import.meta.url / fileURLToPath); tsc shows no new errors
new TeamsAttributor with windowMatch + clusterVote + provisional cluster_id; 22 assertions pass
rewrote handleTeamsAudioData (caption no longer triggers flush); diarizer + attributor singletons wired into Teams pipeline init; no fallback
AudioWorklet lowpass NOT required — bot uses AudioContext({sampleRate:16000}), Chromium handles polyphase resample; note recorded
scripts/bake-diarization-models.js downloads pyannote+wespeaker into transformers.js cache; Dockerfile ts-builder runs it after npm run build; cache flows to runtime via existing node_modules COPY

eval:suite — recall=90.1% purity=95.7% collab=96.9% (realistic noise); pyannote-probe strict@200ms=87.9% vs wespeaker baseline 85.3% (+2.6pp); eval:score recall=90.4% purity=96.2% byte-match prior baseline; transcript=0% due to missing TRANSCRIPTION_API_TOKEN, not regression
docker build vexaai/vexa-bot:0.10.6.2-260530-2244 succeeded; bake step pyannote+wespeaker → /app/vexa-bot/core/node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/.cache (32M verified in runtime layer)
BLOCKED — preflight required interactive transcription-token prompt (no TTY) and stored token returned HTTP 403 (expired). Operator gate.
deploy/lite/Dockerfile.lite gains the same bake-diarization-models step after npm run build

bot+bake portion (steps 1-25/49) PASS; dashboard Next.js prebuild step 39/49 FAILED with module-not-found on route.ts files — pre-existing main-branch issue, out of pack scope (services/dashboard/ untouched). No fresh vexa-lite image produced.
pack-evidence-check.py --mode draft: PASS
pack-evidence-check.py --mode pr-ready: FAIL — missing operator-signed compose/human-eyeball.md, lite/human-eyeball.md, human/overall-functionality.md status:pass + pr.md (now created)
render-pr-body.py wrote .agents/packs/.../pr.md
review.md + code-review.md + tests/README.md + hardenloop/README.md + human/overall-functionality.md written; human verdict files all marked PENDING OPERATOR

BOT_DEV_MODE branch in entrypoint.sh + optional VEXA_BOT_DEV_SRC_MOUNT in runtime-api/profiles.yaml + empty-mount filter in api.py + dev/hot-dev.md + dev/hot-bot.sh; collapses edit-test wall time from ~10min image rebuild to seconds
vexaai/vexa-bot:0.10.6.2-260531-1211 bakes the hot-dev entrypoint
point Compose .env at local-prod transcription LB (http://host.docker.internal:8085) — reuses 4 healthy GPU workers vs spinning up an isolated stack
Docker subnet pool exhausted (5 active pack stacks); pre-created pack-msteams-diar-cutover-compose_vexa on explicit subnet 10.140.65.0/24 instead of touching other stacks
pack-msteams-override.yml drops TTS 127.0.0.1:8002 host port (conflicts with stitched 0.10.6.3 stack) + injects BOT_DEV_MODE/VEXA_BOT_DEV_SRC_MOUNT into runtime-api env

make all PASS (with override); 10 containers healthy; transcription validated against http://172.17.0.1:8085 (local-prod LB, 4 GPU workers, large-v3-turbo); ports 42300/42301/42304/42305/42306/42307/42313/42314 bound; dashboard 200, gateway 200, admin 200
runtime-api recreated with BOT_DEV_MODE=1 and host-source mount path; next bot spawn picks up host code via tsx (no rebuild)

hot-bot.sh probe + docker exec confirms: (1) entrypoint logs 'BOT_DEV_MODE=1 — meeting bot via tsx src/docker.ts', (2) host source mounted at /app/vexa-bot/core/src — docker.ts and index.ts byte sizes match host, diarization/ + teams-attributor.* present, (3) baked ONNX cache present at node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/.cache/onnx-community/, (4) no HF fetch, (5) no caption-flush regression. Per-speaker pipeline init (and diarizer.create) not exercised — that runs on real meeting join, gated on operator-provided Teams URL.
hot-bot.sh now only adds -it when stdin+stdout are both TTYs — works in both interactive and CI/headless contexts
upstream commit fa86a09 not in 0.10.6.2 base — bot's unified-callback.ts lacked X-Internal-Secret header → meeting-api 403. Ported the header mirror; bot joined past callback
hot-dev mount shadows src/; addInitScript looked for src/browser-utils.global.js which only exists in dist/. Added ../dist/ fallback path

tsx (esbuild) inserts __name(...) helper calls that break inside Playwright's page.evaluate serialization → post_join_setup_error. Added __name polyfill via context.addInitScript for hot-dev. Production tsc path doesn't emit __name so the polyfill is a no-op there
meeting-10 listener: diarizer ready, admitted, post-admission OK, audio+caption routing active. Awaiting user audio in meeting

meeting-13 listener (test@vexa.ai user_id=2): pyannote+wespeaker loaded from baked cache; diarizer committed 4× speaker_0; whisper=3 (209ms avg); 1 confirmed segment: 'But it also protects the population because these companies...'. confirm_latency=4.3s. Browser-side audioQueue gating was BLOCKING us — fixed by recording.ts surgery (next op).

deleted audioQueue + speaker-change-flush + text-growth-flush in services/vexa-bot/core/src/platforms/msteams/recording.ts (~80 lines). Browser-side onaudioprocess now dispatches every frame straight to __vexaTeamsAudioData; captions only feed __vexaTeamsCaptionData for the attributor. Banner changed to 'Audio routing active (diarizer-driven, no caption gate)'.

gateway returned 403 'Insufficient scope' for the bot-only token (scopes={bot}); harness-created token (scopes={bot,tx,browser}) returns transcripts correctly. Dashboard sign-in needs the full-scope token.

single-speaker live meetings have no pyannote boundary events, so default maxUtteranceMs=10000 caused 10s commit cadence (felt 'broken' on dashboard). Tuned to maxUtteranceMs=3000 for live; pack synthetic eval re-runs use the default.

original gate (RMS>=0.012, >=600ms speech, >=50% speech_ratio) tuned for clean TTS in RnD eval; silently dropped ALL real Teams audio. Loosened to MIN_TOTAL_SAMPLES>=200ms only. Whisper confidence gates (no_speech_prob/avg_logprob/compression_ratio) remain as tier-2 defence.

meeting-16 produced 1 confirmed segment ('It's very long, but he's talking about in the...' 16.24s) but speakerManager STUCK after — telemetry whisper=1 drafts=1 confirmed=1 frozen for 60+ seconds while diarizer keeps committing every 3s. Likely the discrete commit pattern isn't compatible with speakerManager's continuous-stream session lifecycle. Needs investigation; pack's diarizer+attributor is sound, the audio sink is the bottleneck.

speakerManager confirmed-stuck-after-first-segment was NOT a speakerManager bug. Root cause is in our pack's onCommit drain loop in index.ts: ev.tStartMs/tEndMs are diarizer-session-relative ms (0, 3070, 6140, ...) but teamsPendingFrames[i].ts is Date.now() (wall-clock ~1.7e12). After commit 1 by coincidence drained everything (wall-clock > 3070 always TRUE → break, but other commit-1 frames had been previously consumed), commits 2+ found no frames matching their (session-relative) [tStartMs, tEndMs] window. feedAudio never called → speakerManager idle-trips → emits 1 segment and resets

replaced timestamp-window drain with FIFO drain of wantSamples = round((tEndMs-tStartMs)/1000 * 16000) samples from teamsPendingFrames. The diarizer commits utterances in order so frames for utterance N are the oldest in the buffer. Hot-mount picked it up, no rebuild.

meeting-22: whisper=29, drafts=29, confirmed=13, confirm_latency=4.5s, 9+ confirmed segments streaming to dashboard in real time. Cluster speaker_0 stable. Single-speaker live MS Teams scenario now works end-to-end via the pack's diarizer-driven path
meeting-23: 28 segments, 15/19 key anchors matched (79%), full multilingual coverage (Spanish/French/Portuguese); ALL on speaker_0 cluster (split-issue separate)

Teams branch of speakerManager config: confirmThreshold=1, minAudioDuration=0.5, submitInterval=1. Diarizer-driven commits ARE finalized segment boundaries; streaming-prefix reconfirmation isn't applicable. GoogleMeet path keeps confirmThreshold=2.

OnnxLocalDiarizer.create now passes veryFarThreshold=0.65. With 4s newClusterCooldownMs blocking cluster_1 seed after speaker_0 seeds, this lets very-distant utterances override the cooldown — Maya↔Leo voice difference is ~0.8 wespeaker distance, well above 0.65

Reverted earlier overtune (Teams: confirmThreshold=1, minAudioDuration=0.5, submitInterval=1) back to upstream-main defaults (3/2/2). The pack's surgical delta vs upstream main is just (a) speaker key = diarizer cluster_id instead of caption name, (b) audio-dispatch trigger = pyannote commit instead of caption-flush. Per-speaker buffer + LocalAgreement-2 prefix-confirmation contract is preserved unchanged — when same cluster keeps committing, frames flow continuously into its speakerManager buffer just like upstream's caption-flush bursts do for a named speaker.

Instrumented online-clustering.ts assignWithSeedGate to log per-decision gate values. Observed: nearestDist=0.713 (huge), canSeedNew=true, veryFarOverride=FALSE, allowNewCluster=false → canAllocateNew=false → all utterances stuffed into nearest cluster. Two design bugs: (1) veryFarOverride was gated as `!canSeedNew && nearestDist >= veryFarThreshold` — meaning it could only fire when seed-duration gate was CLOSED, exactly when it would help least. (2) canAllocateNew formula made cooldown a hard gate even for very-far voices: `(canSeedNew||veryFar) && underCap && hasGap && allowNewCluster`.

Fixed in services/vexa-bot/core/src/services/diarization/online-clustering.ts: (1) veryFarOverride = nearestDist >= veryFarThreshold (always evaluated), (2) canAllocateNew = underCap && hasGap && (veryFarOverride || (canSeedNew && allowNewCluster)) — veryFarOverride is now a TRUE bypass of both seed-duration gate and 4s cooldown. Live test on listener-31: dist=0.905, canSeedNew=true, veryFarOverride=true, allowNewCluster=false (cooldown) → ALLOCATE_NEW → speaker_1 seeded, total=2.

Original pyannote-segmenter scanned only freshFrames (~92 frames = last 1.2s of the 10s inference window). When a transition happened earlier (e.g. speaker started 3s ago), the scan missed it — leaving the diarizer to fall back on maxUtteranceMs timer slicing. Now scans the FULL window every inference; lastEmittedBoundaryMs (200ms dedup) prevents re-emitting the same boundary.

Replaced gainsSpeaker (only-additions: silence→speaker, overlap-onset) with speakerSetChanges (any change). speaker_A→speaker_B with no silence gap now correctly emits a boundary and the diarizer splits the utterance at the change-point.
maxUtteranceMs set to 5000 — live UX backstop for long monologues (5s gives perceived-live cadence) while pyannote-driven boundaries carve sub-5s utterances on speaker changes.

meeting-35 dashboard: speaker_0 + speaker_1 + speaker_2 allocated from one user speaking with their voice and playing two different audio clips. Centroid distances 0.959 (clip A vs orig), 0.745 (clip B vs orig). speaker_1 correctly re-recognized when its voice returned (dist 0.184). Full pyannote→cluster→whisper→dashboard pipeline working.

buildTeamsDiarizer closure used `delete require.cache[...]; require('./services/diarization/onnx-local-diarizer')` so SIGUSR1 could pick up algorithm edits. On FIRST build at bot startup this caused @huggingface/transformers' nested onnxruntime-node native binding (.node) to load alongside top-level onnxruntime-node — two ABI-incompatible native libs in the same process. Result: 'libonnxruntime.so.1: version VERS_1.24.3 not found'. Pyannote+wespeaker FATAL'd; PerSpeaker Pipeline init failed; bot joined meeting but had no diarizer → zero commits → zero transcripts. Bot 40 silent. Native modules cannot be hot-reloaded via require.cache invalidation when sibling deps ship their own copy.

index.ts buildTeamsDiarizer() now wraps the existing static OnnxLocalDiarizer.create(...) call — no require.cache surgery. SIGUSR1 can still rebuild the diarizer (resetting clustering state + accepting new constructor args edited in index.ts) but cannot pick up edits inside pyannote-segmenter.ts / online-clustering.ts / onnx-local-diarizer.ts itself. Those still require a bot restart. Cost of native-module safety. Spawned bot 41 for validation.

meeting-41 (test@vexa.ai listener, debug bot, no_one_joined=1h): diarizer ready in 1s after admission. 24 whisper calls / 12 confirmed / confirm_latency=6.2s after ~90s. Two clusters auto-allocated (speaker_0 then speaker_1 at wespeaker dist=0.986, veryFarOverride bypass kicked in past 4s cooldown). Pyannote boundary events firing — speaker→speaker and overlap-onset SPLITs landing; tail samplesIntoUtterance>min/max sometimes SKIP_TOO_LATE which is harmless (diarizer falls back to maxUtteranceMs slicing). Dashboard 41 shows 12 segments correctly attributed across the two clusters

speakerManager minAudioDuration was inherited from upstream CAPTION-driven streaming where audio dripped in continuously and a 3s gate prevented submitting microscopic snippets. In our DIARIZER-driven model the diarizer's own minUtteranceMs=300 already filters short audio; minAudioDuration acted as a redundant filter that starved short turns (Yes/No/back-channels) on bot 46, causing 21/23 cluster-collapse onto speaker_1. Dropped 8→1; idleTimeoutSec 30→5 so short turns that don't hit prefix-confirm still emit quickly.

After prefix-confirm + advanceOffset in speaker-streams.ts handleTranscriptionResult, buffer.lastWords was being set to the FULL currentWords (including already-confirmed words). The trailing unconfirmed audio re-decoded on the next submit and pattern-matched against itself, emitting duplicates (workflow #1 found pairs like 'you don\u0027t' idx 1→2 and 'He might be fighting for that' idx 27→28). Slicing lastWords = currentWords.slice(prefixLen) drops the confirmed words from the baseline so the residual tail cannot self-confirm.

Added updateConfig method on both OnnxLocalDiarizer (maxUtteranceMs/minUtteranceMs/veryFarThreshold/newSpeakerThreshold/cooldown/changePoint thresholds) and SpeakerStreamManager (minAudioDuration/confirmThreshold/maxBufferDuration/idleTimeoutSec). SIGUSR2 handler in index.ts reads /tmp/teams-diarizer-config.json and routes scalar fields under {diarizer:{...},speakerStreams:{...}} keys to the live instances — no rebuild, no readmit.

Discovered the hard way that SIGUSR2 to a node process without a handler triggers Node's default action = TERMINATE. Real signal handlers register inside initPerSpeakerPipeline after Teams admission — any signal arriving before that killed the bot. Added no-op SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 listeners at the TOP of docker.ts (before imports) so signals are always caught; the real handlers add as additional listeners and both fire.

Upstream Teams alone-monitor flips speakersIdentified=true on DOM participant count > 1. The Teams DOM participant selector is fragile and frequently returns 0 even when a human is in the meeting — bots silently dropped 'mid-meeting' via no_one_joined_timeout despite audio + transcripts flowing. Added wall-clock stamping of every non-silent frame to window.__vexaTeamsLastAudioMs in the browser-side audio dispatch; alone-monitor treats audio <3s old as proof of human presence and flips speakersIdentified=true.

SYSTEM_DEFAULTS in meeting-api bumped no_one_joined_timeout 120000→3600000 and max_time_left_alone 900000→3600000. Closes the gap for spawn paths (dashboard UI, skills) that don\u0027t pass an explicit timeout — those paths were defaulting to 2-min no_one_joined and killing bots on the operator before any meaningful test could complete.

Live tests showed cluster MERGES (false negatives — JCAL→CHAMATH handoff, GURLEY interjections) were the dominant failure mode. User explicit preference: false-positive splits over false-negative merges (merged identities unrecoverable; over-split visually messier but audio still attributed to a distinct cluster). Pushed newSpeakerThreshold 0.45→0.30, veryFarThreshold 0.65→0.45, newClusterCooldownMs 4000→1000 — baked into buildTeamsDiarizer defaults so any spawn path inherits them.

pyannoteInferIntervalMs 500→250 (boundary detection latency halved; CPU 10→20%). Dedup windows in pyannote-segmenter.ts: in-batch 100→50ms, ever-emitted 200→100ms — close-spaced boundaries (rapid 'Okay.→Lets be honest...' turn-taking) can both fire within the same inference window.

speaker-streams.ts trySubmit idle branch now checks lastAudioAnyClusterMs (class-level, updated on every feedAudio). When idleMs > idleTimeoutSec AND seamLag = lastAudioAnyClusterMs - buffer.lastAudioTimestamp > 2000ms, the remaining unconfirmed bytes are almost certainly seam audio misrouted to this cluster before pyannote detected the boundary — suppress the emit, fullReset without submitting. Prevents the duplicate-on-wrong-cluster pattern (e.g. 'Okay. Lets be honest...' appearing on speaker_0 after speaker_1 has already taken over with the same content).

Built offline diarization-eval RnD harness (.agents/rnd/diarization-eval): feeds AMI mixed-headset WAV through the EXACT shipping OnnxLocalDiarizer (pyannote+wespeaker) in a vexaai/vexa-bot:dev container, dumps per-utterance embeddings via a new onUtteranceEmbed capture hook, then replays the clustering stage host-side in Python (faithful port of online-clustering.ts) — 57 configs scored in ~0.5s. Capture (the only slow part, ~3min/clip on full cores) is cached; clustering sweeps are instant. Scorer: frame-accuracy (1-DER-confusion via optimal cluster->speaker map), boundary recall/precision, boundary-timing, cluster count. Datasets: AMI EN2002d/IS1009a/TS3003d dense 180s windows + GT diarization + manual word transcripts; range-capable viewer served on :42800.

FOUND via eval: process() maxUtteranceMs cap-commit nulls utteranceStartTs mid-speech; the !wasInSpeech speech-start block doesn't re-run while speech continues, so the next cap-commit stamped utteranceStartMs = utteranceStartTs ?? 0 = tStart=0, stacking commits at the timeline origin (51/74 commits at t=0, union coverage 24s/180s). Insulated in prod because the FIFO drain keys off duration not absolute time, but corrupts any absolute-time consumer + the eval. Fix: re-seed utteranceStartTs=timestampMs after the cap-commit. Result: 1/120 at t=0, coverage 170s/180s, and commit count 74->120 (was losing commits too).

THE real cause of the live speaker-collapse/merge. minSeedUtteranceMs=3000 requires an utterance >=3s to SEED a new cluster. Eval found rapid-conversation clips are ~100% utterances <3s (TS3003d median 0.72s, 100% <3s) -> nothing seeds -> entire meeting collapses to speaker_0 (TS3003d: exactly 1 cluster, boundary recall 0.015). The live All-In interruption merges (JCAL->CHAMATH not splitting) were this, NOT a threshold problem — the earlier Fix-7 newSpeakerThreshold 0.30 fought the symptom and caused runaway over-clustering. Fix: minSeedUtteranceMs 3000->1000. TS3003d 1->multi cluster, clean-speech acc 0.21->0.87; IS1009a bRecall 0.51->0.77.

Measured wespeaker embedding distances directly on GT-labeled utterances across 3 AMI clips: same-speaker cosine 0.44-0.60 (p75 ~0.54-0.61), diff-speaker 0.85-0.94 (p25 ~0.82-0.88) — clean consistent gap, 0% overlap. Shipped default newSpeakerThreshold 0.45 sat INSIDE the same-speaker spread (~40% of one speaker's utts exceed it -> duplicate clusters, the 'speaker_1 scattered across C/E/A/E' failure). Fix-7's 0.30 was far worse (15-28 clusters). Reset to nst 0.55 / vft 0.80 (in the empty gap). cooldown 1000->2000. Applied all to index.ts buildTeamsDiarizer; reverts the backwards Fix-7 live-tune.

Memory: node baseline 40MiB; +pyannote+wespeaker+onnxruntime = 337MiB loaded, ~355MiB peak => net extra ~300MiB (dominated by ORT arenas, not weights). CPU: 1-core 0.05x realtime (19.6 core-s/audio-s); ~96-core fast-feed 1.2x realtime; ~15 cores busy flat-out => one live stream needs ~12-16 cores headroom, almost all pyannote (4 infers/s on 10s windows at 250ms cadence). pyannoteInferIntervalMs 250->500 ~halves it. onnxruntime ignores cpuset (pthread_setaffinity errors) and spawns ~128 threads/container -> the parallel-eval thrash; needs explicit ORT thread cap to pack streams per box.

Operator flagged the validation subset as too noisy. Confirmed: I had picked the DENSEST windows (max speaker-changes) which also maximizes overlap (EN2002d dense=48% simultaneous speech). Rotated to the CLEANEST low-overlap windows (2-6% overlap, still 15-26 turn-changes) — Teams-representative. On clean audio + applied config, clean-speech attribution accuracy is 0.84-0.91 across all 3 clips (IS1009a 0.84, EN2002d 0.91, TS3003d 0.88) — the pipeline is solid; the dense windows were sandbagging it. frameAcc 0.48-0.77 still penalized by ~2x over-clustering (5-8 clusters vs 3-4 GT).

FOUND via clean rotation: the diarizer VAD gate compared frame RMS against the ABSOLUTE speechRms=0.012 threshold. The EN2002d clean window is 10x quieter (mean RMS 0.0028) so only 2% of frames cleared the gate — 98% of 143s of real speech silently dropped (degenerate 4 commits). Same failure on quiet Teams conference audio (distant speaker/low gain). Fix: track a running noise floor (asymmetric EMA, falls fast toward quiet frames, rises slowly) and gate RELATIVE to it: dynSpeech=max(speechRms*0.3, noiseFloor*3.5), dynSilence=max(silenceRms*0.3, noiseFloor*2.0). Validated: EN2002d 4 -> 71 commits, clean-speech accuracy 0.91. Normal-level audio (IS1009a) unchanged.

Joint re-tune on the 3 clean low-overlap windows (clean-speech accuracy objective, instant replay). Winner raised veryFarThreshold 0.80->0.90 (cuts spurious very-far-bypass clusters) and minSeedUtteranceMs 1000->1500. Final shipping config: maxUtteranceMs3000, pyannoteInferIntervalMs250, newSpeakerThreshold0.55, veryFarThreshold0.90, newClusterCooldownMs2000, minSeedUtteranceMs1500, + adaptive VAD. Validated clean-speech accuracy: IS1009a 0.87, EN2002d 0.91, TS3003d 0.81 (mean ~0.86); cluster counts EN2002d 4(GT3), TS3003d 5(GT4), IS1009a 8(GT3 - residual over-cluster). Boundary recall 0.33-0.69 remains the weak spot (segmentation-limited).

The adaptive noise-floor VAD (8f44e7a) was a LIVE REGRESSION. On normal-level Teams audio it lowered the effective speech gate (dynSpeech=max(speechRms*0.3, noiseFloor*3.5) ~0.0036 floor vs the absolute 0.012), so the bot's natural pauses never released inSpeech. The diarizer never idled → the per-frame change-point check + 250ms peek-embed + pyannote inference ran CONTINUOUSLY → 55-108 CPU cores per stream, growing transcript lag, and unbounded per-speaker buffer 'garbage buildup'. Reverted to the fixed absolute-threshold gate. Confirmed on bot-61: CPU idles to 23% on audio pauses (was pinned), confirm_latency stable 4.4-5.3s (not growing), realtime pace held, transcript coherent. The quiet-audio drop the adaptive VAD targeted was a pathological AMI eval artifact, not a real Teams failure mode; gain-normalization belongs upstream if ever needed.
